name: Housing Foundation Of America, Ft. Lauderdale Branch
description_short: HUD-certified non-profit offering free homebuyer counseling, workshops, and financial education across South Florida and North Carolina.
description_long:
Housing Foundation of America, Inc. is a HUD-approved 501(c)(3) non-profit organization that has operated for 10 years, providing housing-related assistance and financial education to consumers. The organization specializes in pre-purchase and post-purchase homebuyer counseling, with HUD-certified counselors delivering services across multiple counties in Florida and North Carolina. Their primary mission centers on helping consumers build credit, manage budgets, understand homeownership responsibilities, and access down payment assistance programs. The organization operates as a legitimate non-profit with transparent operations and community partnerships including lenders, realtors, and local builders.

HFA offers a comprehensive range of free and low-cost services including HUD-certified homebuyer workshops, 8-hour certificate courses, credit and budget counseling, foreclosure prevention assistance, homebuyer tools, and reverse mortgage counseling. They provide both in-person (now virtual) workshops and online courses through their eHome America platform. The organization maintains an active client portal for down payment assistance alerts and offers specialized programming like landlord certification and minority builders coalition partnerships. Their workshops cover essential topics including establishing and rebuilding credit, the basics of saving, and homebuyer essentials.

What distinguishes HFA is their HUD certification, which indicates compliance with federal counseling standards and access to official homebuyer education frameworks like Fannie Mae's guidelines. They serve multiple geographic areas across Florida (Miami-Dade, Broward, Palm Beach, St. Lucie, Martin, Brevard, Lee, Orange counties) and North Carolina (Guilford, Mecklenburg, Forsyth, Wake), suggesting established regional presence. Their dual focus on credit counseling and homeownership education addresses both foundational financial skills and housing-specific needs, rather than single-issue services.

The organization appears legitimate and well-established, though their website notes it is "not activated," which may indicate technical issues or a recent migration. Their content is extensive and service-focused, with active social media presence (YouTube channel, podcast), upcoming workshops, and regular blog updates. For consumers seeking free, non-profit guidance on homebuying, credit rebuilding, and mortgage counseling, HFA represents a credible resource with institutional backing.
pros: HUD-certified counselors ensuring compliance with federal counseling standards
Completely free services as a 501(c)(3) non-profit with no financial incentive conflicts
Multi-state presence across 13+ counties in Florida and North Carolina
Comprehensive curriculum including 8-hour certificate courses using Fannie Mae framework
Specialized foreclosure prevention counseling and loss mitigation support
Multiple service delivery methods including virtual ZOOM workshops and online eHome America courses
Down payment assistance program alerts and client portal access
cons: Website displays 'not activated' message, suggesting technical issues or outdated platform
Limited specific details about counselor qualifications, response times, or service availability
Geographic service limitations may not extend to all U.S. states
No clear information about waitlist times, appointment scheduling, or language accessibility
Website structure appears repetitive with navigation duplication, reducing usability
best_for: First-time homebuyers seeking HUD-certified education and pre-purchase counseling
Consumers rebuilding credit and needing non-profit budgeting assistance
Homeowners facing foreclosure or mortgage delinquency seeking prevention resources
Individuals interested in reverse mortgage counseling and options
diagnosis: Best for first-time homebuyers and credit-rebuilding consumers who qualify for free HUD-certified counseling and want institutional, non-profit guidance without sales pressure. Primary caveat: Website technical issues and limited appointment availability details make it necessary to contact them directly to confirm current service access and scheduling.
services: Foreclosure prevention and loss mitigation counseling
Reverse mortgage counseling and HECM workshops
Home repair tools and home inspection resources
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
address: 2101 N Andrews Ave, Wilton Manors, FL 33311
